what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger record describing the /home/tim/vllm-tests directory, documenting its actual role as a live dependency for two production model-serving services and its relationships to environment files and other ledger entries.
observations: Frontmatter marks it as a descriptive terrain-entry named vllm-tests with status unconfirmed.
It describes a 1.3M / 40-file external engine directory at /home/tim/vllm-tests.
Two live services use launch scripts inside this folder: vllm-jina-v4.service runs serve_jina_v4.sh on port 8002, and llama-swap routes qwen2b/qwen0.8b through serve_2b.sh.
It contains model-serve scripts, benchmark scripts, ollama modelfiles, reference docs, a vllm-stack subdir, a shared chat template, and Company UI screenshots.
It links to related ledger entries via wikilinks: [[llama-swap]], [[jina-v4-env]], [[vllm-env]], and [[company]].
Coverage metadata says only 8 of 40 files have been read, with no git remote.
The body explicitly corrects the 'tests/scratch' framing and labels the folder as load-bearing production infrastructure.
standouts: The folder name 'vllm-tests' contradicts its documented role as production infrastructure.
Systemd service vllm-jina-v4 and llama-swap both depend on scripts located here.
It mixes live launch scripts, benchmarks, modelfiles, and UI screenshots in one directory.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with ledger metadata such as type, register, status, coverage, ports, models, and service_units.
References related ledger entries with [[wikilink]] syntax in the body and frontmatter.
Includes an evidence/notes section that explicitly revises the initial framing.
Labels external directory as an engine kind with descriptive register.
concerns: Status is 'unconfirmed' even though the entry asserts live production use.
Only 8 of 40 files have been read, leaving most contents undocumented.
Folder name suggests disposable tests, creating deletion risk for load-bearing scripts.
No git remote is recorded, so version history and recovery are unclear.
Absolute home-directory path /home/tim/vllm-tests ties it to a specific user environment.
notes: Treat this entry as a warning that /home/tim/vllm-tests is live infrastructure, not scratch, because two production services launch from scripts inside it.
questions: What do the remaining 32 unread files contain?

purpose_vs_actual: The file's name and initial 'tests' framing imply scratch/experimental content, but the entry itself documents that it is live production infrastructure for embeddings and small-model serving.
intention_signals: The entry appears to be trying to correct a misleading label by proving that vllm-tests is a load-bearing production dependency and mapping exactly which services, ports, and environment files rely on it.
novelty: A directory named 'tests' that is not scratch but hosts the launch scripts for live systemd and llama-swap services, while also storing benchmark artifacts and UI screenshots as historical documentation.
contribution: It contributes a corrected, file-local description of a live model-serving substrate, including launch-script paths, port assignments, model names, environment couplings, and an explicit warning that deleting or neglecting this folder would break production services.
summary_for_embedding: A terrain ledger entry for /home/tim/vllm-tests, a 1.3M directory of model-serve scripts, benchmarks, and screenshots that is not scratch but a live dependency for vllm-jina-v4 and llama-swap small-model services.
intention_for_embedding: To document and warn that the vllm-tests folder is production infrastructure serving embeddings and small models via systemd and llama-swap.
